name: Pear Orchard
description_short: Oriana's Orchard & Nursery sells fruit trees and fresh organic produce, specializing in Asian pears for the Chicago area. This is a horticultural business, not a consumer finance company.
description_long:
Oriana's Orchard & Nursery is a fruit tree and produce business based in Skokie, Illinois, serving the Chicagoland and Midwest regions. What began as a hobby has evolved into a commercial operation focused on growing and selling specialty fruit trees and fresh produce. The business was established by 2015 and continues operating as of 2021.

The company offers bare root fruit trees (2-3 years old) available for pickup in March through April at the owner's Skokie home, as well as at farmer's markets and community events throughout the greater Chicagoland area. They specialize in Asian pear trees but also grow pawpaws, persimmons, and other lesser-known fruit varieties. The owner provides supplementary services including on-site home orchard consultation, tree espalier training, and custom grafting. Fresh produce including USDA Organic Certified Asian pears, pawpaws, and persimmons are sold seasonally at farmer's markets.

The company distinguishes itself through rigorous varietal testing—the owner has tested over 60 fruit tree and plant varieties and selected only those suited to Northern Illinois' harsh climate. All trees are cold-hardy, tested at the western Illinois growing site where temperatures reach -30°F (and -50°F in 2019). The focus is on superior taste, disease resistance, and ease of cultivation for home growers. The Asian pears are USDA Organic Certified, indicating compliance with federal organic standards.

This is a legitimate horticultural business with no connection to consumer finance. It has been miscategorized in the CreditDoc database. The company operates transparently with contact information, a physical location, and seasonal availability clearly stated. However, it serves an entirely different market—home gardeners and fruit enthusiasts—not consumers seeking financial products or services.
services: Bare root fruit tree sales (2-3 year old plants)
Asian pear tree specialization and sales
Fresh USDA Organic Certified Asian pears (seasonal)
pros: USDA Organic Certified Asian pears, meeting federal organic production standards
Extensive varietal testing (60+ varieties) ensures trees are suited to Northern Illinois climate and conditions
Cold-hardiness proven in extreme conditions (-30°F to -50°F winters at growing site)
Offers specialized services including orchard consultation, espalier training, and custom grafting
Trees are 2-3 years old at sale, reducing time to first fruit production for buyers
Direct pickup and farmer's market availability provide multiple purchasing options
Selection criteria prioritize taste quality, disease resistance, and low-maintenance growing
cons: Bare root trees only available March-April, limiting purchasing windows
In-person pickup required at Skokie location; no mail-order shipping option stated
Limited product information on website regarding specific varieties, pricing, or tree sizes
Seasonal availability of fresh produce limits year-round purchasing
No online ordering or e-commerce functionality mentioned on website
best_for: Chicago-area homeowners seeking cold-hardy fruit trees adapted to Midwest climate
Home gardeners interested in specialty fruits like Asian pears, pawpaws, and persimmons
Consumers seeking organically-grown fresh fruit during farmer's market season
diagnosis: This company is a horticultural nursery and produce grower, not a consumer finance entity. It has been incorrectly categorized as a payday-alternative lender. Immediate recategorization or removal from CreditDoc is necessary, as it provides no financial services whatsoever.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
